text: if u are dying instantly, u arent going to magically not die by swapping agents. if u just want some duelists to use, i recommend trying out reyna/jett/phoenix. they have ways to play aggro and escape, or ways to get easier kills or recover hp. but i dont think swappin agents is what u should focus on. imo get all ur experience in tdm/dm with some of those agents, u will get into way more fights to have more experience than u would in ranked. get into as many gunfights as u can. if u get 10 kills and die 20 times, thats much better than camping and killing 10 ppl with 5 deaths bc u got into way more fights which helps u practice more and get rid of fight anxiety. then in ranked, play what u prefer, cypher plays can be cool, watch some of the cypher youtube nerds that post cool vids and check their playstyle out. also playing cypher will open u up to lurk timings, u dont only need to rely on cage/trip kills with him. just my opinion tho!

text: It has a lot of YouTube subscribers but in the esports sphere C9 and 100T are far more recognizable than LOUD. Yes, LOUD has many Brazilian fans, but even a majority of their videos that attribute to their YT fandom are all soccer videos, very little pertain to esports. LOUD currently exists in only 3 esports, two of them are actually large esports, and they don’t cater to non Portuguese speaking communities. C9 and 100T on the other hand are recognizable brands in many esports all across the board and have fans all over the world. C9 for example has 15 esports division. Does LOUD have a lot of followers on social media? Yeah, in fact they have the second highest social media following of anyone in the world. But that’s not indicative of their fanbase in esports as a whole. Just because they have a lot of Valorant fans does mean they have the most fans across the esports sphere, it’s not cumulative.
label: constructive
